config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah jadi alasan untuk menekan kebebasan, martabat, atau keselamatan orang per orang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tapi seharusnya dicapai lewat aturan yang adil dan proporsional, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak dasar seenaknya. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari perlindungan kuat terhadap individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The rights of the individual should not be treated as secondary to society’s interests; in the United States, I see individual rights as the main guardrail against abuse by majorities, governments, and institutions. That said, rights are not absolute in every situation, because public safety, disease control, and basic order sometimes require limits that are narrow and justified. What I reject is the idea that “society” is automatically a higher value, because that can become a convenient excuse to silence minorities or override liberty without real accountability. A healthy society should prove its case before restricting a person, not assume the person must yield by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, dari kebebasan berpendapat sampai hak kelompok minoritas, padahal seharusnya negara yang membuktikan bahwa pembatasan itu benar-benar perlu dan proporsional. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru lebih kuat kalau dibangun di atas perlindungan hak dasar, bukan dengan menjadikan individu sebagai pihak yang selalu harus mengalah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to justify overreach by governments, majorities, and institutions. I do think there are narrow exceptions where public safety or emergency conditions justify limited restrictions, but those limits have to be specific, temporary, and heavily accountable. If “society” becomes a blank check, then minorities and unpopular individuals are the first people to lose protection. To me, a society is strongest when it proves it can protect people’s rights even when it is inconvenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang tidak perlu dan kadang menekan kelompok yang lemah atau minoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dibangun di atas perlindungan hak individu yang jelas, terutama hak untuk berpendapat, berbeda, dan tidak diperlakukan sewenang-wenang. Kalau negara atau mayoritas diberi prioritas otomatis, yang sering terjadi bukan solidaritas, melainkan penyalahgunaan kuasa dengan bahasa yang terdengar mulia. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang berani membatasi diri sendiri saat ingin membatasi hak orang lain.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From an American perspective, that framing makes me wary because it has historically been used to excuse majority rule, government overreach, and the quiet erosion of minority protections. I do think limited restrictions can be justified for things like public safety or emergencies, but only when they are narrow, evidence-based, and accountable. If society can override rights whenever it claims a good reason, then those rights are not really rights at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
